title: Dielectric Cure Monitoring Ion Viscosity and Degree of Cure
content: Dielectric analysis (DEA) tracks epoxy cure state by measuring ion viscosity (IV) via interdigitated sensors excited at 1–10 kHz. Degree of cure α = (log IV_max − log IV) / (log IV_max − log IV_min). Gelation occurs at α ≈ 0.55 (steep IV rise); vitrification at α ≈ 0.85 (IV plateau). Full cure threshold α ≥ 0.95 triggers controlled cool-down. Sensor signal correlates with DSC-measured conversion with R² > 0.97 for standard epoxy-amine systems.
